mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations and reduces corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained employment growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following strong PAYEMS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which reduces equity risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1.5% MoM AND HOUST declines > 5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 705,180M and HOUST at 1,360K as of 2024-04-15
regime_hint: demand-side recession or sharp demand shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below the 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines below MA are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk, which triggers equity risk-off behavior and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE spikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL growth > 3% YoY) and restrictive federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals real monetary tightening that suppresses equity valuations through both higher discount rates and reduced earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 313.225 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-05-15
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with persistent inflation and tight policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured on a quarter-over-quarter basis above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following strong GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,200 thousand units annualized while simultaneously retail sales (RSAFS) growth slows to below 2% YoY signals a synchronized demand shock across residential and consumer sectors, which reduces forward equity returns over the next 20 trading days through weakened economic momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,360K and RSAFS at 705,180M as of 2024-04-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand weakness across housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
